free_text: Target residues from the interaction of copper with
15-45 MeV/nucleon 12C ions.
Atlas Facility used to repeat
experiment at 180 MeV.
Beam energies given are for energies at center of
targets.
Irradiated for either 0.5 or 3.5 hours.
No information on decay data.
Tabulated uncertainties are the larger of
standard deviation in the mean value and estimated
uncertainty of the individual determinations.
The latter are based on the propagation of the errors
in the fits and, in addition, include:
a 5% uncertainty in detector efficiencies.
An additional 5% uncertainty has been folded
into the overall uncertainty of cross sections based
on a single determination of a single gamma-ray.
Data taken from Table I of PR/C,39,2227,1989
SD
SD: In Subent 006 value in DATA section
without heading deleted. ERR-ANALYS was upgraded.
OS. ISOMER coding corrected in subs.2,3
OS. Subs. 6,7 split in two; REACTION in
subs. 4,5 modified
OG. REACTION updated in subs.006-007.
Atlas Facility
Target stacks containing 20.7 millig/cm**2 copper foil
surrounded by carbon foils.
Samples assayed at Purdue beginning several hours
after irradiations.
ISOMER column deleted
Target stacks containing 20.7 millig/cm**2 copper foil
surrounded by Mylar forward and backward catcher
foils.
Samples from short irradiations assayed for about 1 d.
Those from longer irradiations were assayed starting
1 d after irradiation and continuing for several
months.
